violations.text: An employee was observed touching ready to eat food (gluten-free toast for an egg sandwich) with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. Food items were disposed of during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/17/2026

Raw animal foods (such as shell eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as prepared chicken and roasted veggies) in the 1-door reach-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Food items were rearranged during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/17/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to prevent the accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/17/2026

The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by kitchen utensils in a pan. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Pan of utensils were removed from sink basin and moved onto prep unit cutting board. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/21/2025

Various utensils were observed stored in a pan of stagnant water between uses. Utensils were removed from pan of water during time of inspection. Utensils must be kept on a dry clean surface, in water of 135 DF or more, or water with sufficient flow to flush away any source of contamination. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/21/2025
